Tutorial: Creating Documentation with RullstPress

RullstPress is the native Static Site Generator (SSG) built into the Rullst CLI. If you know how to write Markdown files, you already know how to use RullstPress.

1. Folder Structure

To start writing documentation for your Rullst project (or any other project), just create a docs/ folder at the root of your repository:

my_project/
├── docs/
│   ├── index.md           # Your Landing Page (Special Hero section)
│   ├── getting-started.md # A starter guide
│   └── architecture.md    # Technical documentation
├── src/
├── Cargo.toml
└── Rullst.toml

2. Local Development Server

To preview your documentation in real-time as you write, open your terminal at the project root and run:

cargo rullst docs dev

This will compile your .md files and start a fast, secure server on port 4000. Go to http://localhost:4000 in your browser. The server automatically maps routes based on your file names. For example, getting-started.md becomes /getting-started.html.

3. The Landing Page (index.md)

RullstPress has a special rule: the index.md file is treated differently!

Instead of getting a sidebar navigation, it renders a special Landing Page layout. The Markdown content you write in index.md will be hidden in the HTML, and in its place, a beautiful Hero (Titles, Subtitles, and CTA Buttons) and a Features list will appear.

4. Production Build

When you finish writing and want to publish your site (like on GitHub Pages, Vercel, or Netlify), simply run:

cargo rullst docs build

This will generate all final production-ready HTML files in the docs/dist/ folder. Just grab this folder and host it wherever you like!

It's that simple. Focus on the content, and let Rullst handle the Premium design.
